conditions.critical_component: PIB-diol, PIB-monool, PIB without OH functionality
conditions.evidence_text: LCCC was conducted on a Waters HPLC system with Waters 2695 separation module at a flow rate of 0.5 mL/min. For UV/RI system, a Waters 2414 Refractive Index detector at 30°C was used along with a Waters 2996 Photodiode Array detector operating at λ = 254 nm. 10 μL samples of 0.5 wt% polymer solutions were injected for UV/RI system. All measurements were carried out at a constant column temperature of 35°C and constant sample temperature of 30°C. One reversed phase C18 chromatography column (YMC HPLC COLUMN) with average particle size = 5 μm, pore diameters = 120 A˚ and column size (Inner diameter × Length) = 4.6 mm × 250 mm was used. HPLC grade THF and methanol were used to prepare the mobile phase and the solution of the polymers. The solvents were freshly distilled before use to avoid any degassing related issue. The critical solvent composition for PIB was established as THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w). Premixing of the mobile phase by weight is necessary for a constant and exact composition. Samples were prepared by dissolving polymer in THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w).
conditions.notes: The critical condition was established for PIB samples of varying functionality but similar MW (approximately 2000). The RI detector emerged as a better concentration detector under LCCC condition.
